title: Server Configuration, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, TLS, and Graceful Shutdown · Contract Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective explicit http.Server protocol and shutdown configuration; retain the failing request and response, isolate the first causal server boundary, and keep a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in explicit http.Server protocol and shutdown configuration and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Configure ReadHeaderTimeout, body strategy, WriteTimeout, IdleTimeout, MaxHeaderBytes, and per-handler deadlines from threat and workload evidence.
Configure Server.Protocols for HTTP/1 and TLS HTTP/2, treat UnencryptedHTTP2 as explicit h2c authority, and name HTTP/3 as outside net/http server support.
Set HTTP2Config stream, frame, compression-table, flow-control, ping, write-byte, and error-count boundaries without confusing transport and server fields.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Shutdown Close Register
steps.instruction:
Order the Shutdown Close Register Go server HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

content.text: A debug team handles case 3681 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 service by timeout and graceful-drain policy while excluding HTTP/3; then cancel during the dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use Shutdown deadlines, readiness withdrawal, listener stop, in-flight drain, hijacked-connection handling, RegisterOnShutdown, Close fallback, and exit evidence.

Competency probe: Use Shutdown deadlines, readiness withdrawal, listener stop, in-flight drain, hijacked-connection handling, RegisterOnShutdown, Close fallback, and exit evidence.

For Go server Publish the incident record · Debug Shutdown Close Register, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Server.Shutdown waits for hijacked connections and guarantees every cleanup callback has finished.
